title: By Senator Rosilicie Ochoa Bogh, 23rd Senate District

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Jennifer Doudna and Emmanuelle Charpentier's 2012 discovery of CRISPR gene-editing technology enabled scientists to develop vaccines for COVID-19 quickly.",
    "Women and girls make up half of the world’s population but represent a low percentage in STEM fields.",
    "Less than 30% of researchers worldwide are women according to UNESCO.",
    "Women represent only 26% of the STEM workforce in the U.S. despite making up nearly half of the working population.",
    "Women in STEM professions earn one-third more than women in non-STEM-related occupations.",
    "Employment opportunities in science and engineering-related fields have been growing faster than all other occupations over the past decade.",
    "In California, only 15% of engineering graduates and only 15% of engineers in the workforce are women.",
    "Women earn nearly 60% of bachelor’s degrees but are underrepresented in STEM-related college degrees, especially in computer sciences and engineering fields.",
    "STEM careers represent some of the highest-paying positions in any field."
  ]
}
```
